name: Photo Editorial
tagline: Photography magazine editorial style with stark black/white structure, oversized headlines, and hard color-block mosaics.

palette.description: Black-and-white editorial backbone as the professional anchor; three hard color blocks (sky-blue, orange-red, golden-orange) for feature section mosaics — used as large-area backgrounds, not accents; real photography is the primary visual content.

typography.style: Ultra-bold all-caps sans-serif for display and section headlines. Tight letter-spacing (tracking). Magazine-poster feeling. Body text small and information-dense. No decorative fonts, no serifs.

best_for: Photography tools, creator communities, visual product platforms, creative professionals. When real photography can carry the visual work. Editorial-graphic design reviews, portfolio platforms, creator monetization tools.
avoid_for: B2B enterprise, quiet minimalist aesthetics, personal narratives without strong visual material. Don't use if you can't supply strong photographic content.
anti_patterns: replacing real photography with illustrations — Photo Editorial's quality signal is the photography itself
softening the color blocks into gradients — hard-edged color blocks are the structural vocabulary
changing headlines from all-caps bold to softer type — the poster-headline feel is the identity
using the color blocks as small accents rather than full section backgrounds
